precedents.name: India Culture Week at Seattle University
partners: Seattle University Office of Global Engagement
precedents.scope: 3-day multi-disciplinary program: Indian classical and folk dance, food festival, yoga, arts workshops, photo exhibit, library gift
precedents.headlinePerformance: 'Natyam: A Dance Mosaic of Bharat' by Prashanthi Chitre Institute of Performing Arts
precedents.whyItMatters: The single most important precedent for our festival. CGI Seattle actively wants to replicate this format. The multi-disciplinary India Week format is now a known template.
applicableLessons: CGI Seattle is the active driver
US university is the institutional anchor
Roundglass India Center is the proven intermediary

precedents.name: Swami Vivekananda Monument Unveiling
precedents.whyItMatters: Demonstrates ICCR gifts cultural monuments/assets to US cities with credible co-presenters. Shows the scale of ICCR's West Coast investment.
applicableLessons: City government is engageable for major Indian cultural moments

precedents.name: Diwali: Lights of India
precedents.scope: 30+ Indian performing arts groups in one day — Bharatanatyam, Kathak, Odissi, Manipuri, regional folk forms
precedents.model: Free community festival; strong municipal backing
precedents.whyItMatters: Shows the density of Indian performing arts groups already operating in Seattle. Also shows that 4Culture and Seattle Center are experienced funders/venues for Indian programming.
applicableLessons: Many local Indian performing arts groups exist
4Culture and Seattle Center are reliable infrastructure

precedents.name: First Baisakhi Celebration at Washington State Capitol
precedents.whyItMatters: Demonstrates state-government access. State-level proclamations or recognitions are achievable asks for our festival.

precedents.name: India Showcased at Asia Pacific Cultural Center
precedents.whyItMatters: Tacoma venue option if Seattle proper is full; APCC is experienced Asian-cultural presenter.

precedents.name: Seattle Symphony — Songs and Dances of India
precedents.scope: Symphony programming including Reena Esmail compositions and Bollywood classics
precedents.whyItMatters: Major US classical institution is open to Indian programming. Seattle Symphony is a potential co-presenter for festival classical music component.

precedents.name: First India Day Parade, Seattle
precedents.whyItMatters: Consulate investment in pan-Indian cultural diversity, not just Bollywood/Hindi-centric programming.

precedents.name: Ali Akbar College of Music
precedents.scope: Oldest Hindustani classical music institution in North America
precedents.whyItMatters: Institutional foundation we can partner with; potential artist source, faculty as experts for P-3 visa support letters.

precedents.name: The Music Circle (LA)
precedents.scope: Organizes North and South Indian classical music seasons
precedents.whyItMatters: Direct precedent for fundable Indian classical music programming model. Top Year 2 LA co-presenter candidate.

precedents.name: CGI SF Swami Vivekananda Musical Tribute
precedents.location: Center for the Performing Arts, San Jose
precedents.scope: Top-tier classical Indian artist slate at major Bay Area venue
precedents.whyItMatters: Proves CGI SF will activate major venues and top artists. Directly replicable — this is our aspirational format.

precedents.name: Chhandam School of Kathak / Chitresh Das Dance Company
precedents.whyItMatters: Major Kathak institution; potential artist/faculty partner.

precedents.name: Cleveland Thyagaraja Aradhana Festival
precedents.visaPrecedent: Has used P-3 artist visas annually; demonstrates that small dance schools with minimal staff can successfully petition for Indian artists
precedents.whyItMatters: De-risks the P-3 pathway. If a small dance school in Cleveland can do it, so can we.
